title: A Systematic Study On Phytochemical Screening And Antioxidant Potential Of Sansevieria Zeylanica (L.) WILLD With Quantification Of Major Bioactive Constituents
author: Jaradi Parthkumar*, Maitreya Anusha, Pandya Himanshu, Solanki Hitesh
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The present research focused on the analysis of phytochemical constituents and antioxidant activity of  Sansevieria zeylanica  leaves using methanolic and aqueous extracts. The extraction process showed that the aqueous extract produced a higher yield (29.69%) compared to the methanolic extract (21.29%), indicating better extraction of polar compounds in water. Preliminary phytochemical screening confirmed the presence of various secondary metabolites including flavonoids, phenols, tannins, glycosides, steroids, and terpenoids in both extracts, whereas alkaloids were predominantly detected in the methanolic extract. This suggests that methanol has a greater capacity to extract a broader range of phytochemicals. Further quantitative analysis demonstrated that the methanolic extract contained higher levels of total flavonoids (6.84±0.206 mg QE/g), total phenols (0.37±0.01 mg GAE/g), and total tannins (13.18±3.89 mg TAE/g) compared to the aqueous extract, which showed lower values (0.24±0.03 mg GAE/g for phenols and 0.82±0.25 mg TAE/g for tannins). These findings indicate that methanol is more efficient in extracting antioxidant-related compounds. The antioxidant potential was assessed using the DPPH radical scavenging method with ascorbic acid as a standard reference. The IC₅₀ values obtained were 921.324 µg/mL for the methanolic extract and 11211.5 µg/mL for the aqueous extract, which are significantly higher than that of ascorbic acid (547.619 µg/mL), indicating weak antioxidant activity. At the highest tested concentration, the methanolic extract showed 42.3±0.95% inhibition, while the aqueous extract exhibited only 5.12±0.67% inhibition.

keywords: Sansevieria zeylanica, phytochemical screening, maceration method, DPPH assay, quantitative analysis.
